data-structures.title: Data Structures
data-structures.explanation: Fundamental building blocks for managing data efficiently.

algorithms.title: Algorithms
algorithms.explanation: Step-by-step logic for problem-solving and efficiency analysis.

operating-systems.title: Operating Systems
operating-systems.explanation: The interface between hardware and the end-user.

dbms.title: DBMS
dbms.explanation: Structured systems for large-scale data storage and retrieval.

architecture.title: Architecture (COA)
architecture.explanation: Hard-core systems design and instruction execution.

mcqs.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of QuickSort?
mcqs.explanation: Worst case occurs when the pivot is the smallest or largest element, leading to n^2 comparisons.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT an ACID property in DBMS?
mcqs.explanation: ACID stands for Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability.

mcqs.question: In Operating Systems, what does 'Starvation' imply?
mcqs.explanation: Starvation happens when a low-priority process waits indefinitely for a resource grabbed by high-priority processes.

mcqs.question: Which layer of the OSI model handles error-free delivery of data frames?
mcqs.explanation: Data Link layer is responsible for node-to-node framing and error detection.

mcqs.question: What is the maximum number of nodes in a binary tree of height 'h'?
mcqs.explanation: A full binary tree of height h (root at 0) has 2^(h+1)-1 nodes.

mcqs.question: Which sorting algorithm is stable and has O(n log n) worst-case complexity?
mcqs.explanation: MergeSort is always O(n log n) and maintains the relative order of equal elements (stable).

mcqs.question: What is the primary objective of Caching?
mcqs.explanation: Caching stores frequently accessed data in faster memory (SRAM) to reduce latency.

mcqs.question: What does 'BCNF' stand for in DBMS?
mcqs.explanation: BCNF is a stricter version of 3NF where every determinant is a candidate key.

mcqs.question: In TCP, what is 'Three-Way Handshake' used for?
mcqs.explanation: TCP uses SYN, SYN-ACK, and ACK to establish a reliable connection.

mcqs.question: Which CPU Scheduling algorithm can lead to Convoy Effect?
mcqs.explanation: FCFS (First-Come, First-Served) can cause shorter processes to wait behind long ones (Convoy Effect).

mcqs.question: A B-Tree of order 'm' can have at most how many children?
mcqs.explanation: By definition, a B-tree of order m can have a maximum of m children per node.

mcqs.question: Which register holds the address of the next instruction to be executed?
mcqs.explanation: The PC (Program Counter) tracks the next instruction's address in memory.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of 'Virtual Memory'?
options: Enable large processes in small RAM
mcqs.explanation: Virtual Memory creates an illusion of large memory by using disk space as secondary storage.

mcqs.question: Which protocol is used for email retrieval?
mcqs.explanation: POP3 (Post Office Protocol) or IMAP is used to retrieve emails; SMTP is used to send them.

mcqs.question: If a graph has 'n' vertices, what is the maximum number of edges in a simple undirected graph?
mcqs.explanation: nC2 = n(n-1)/2 edges for a complete undirected graph.

formulas.title: Pipelining Execution
formulas.content: ET_pipe = (k + n - 1) * Clock_cycle. Speedup S = n*k / (k + n - 1).

formulas.title: Master Theorem
formulas.content: T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n). Comparison of n^log_b(a) with f(n).

formulas.title: Networking: Channel Capacity
formulas.content: Shannon's: C = B * log_2(1 + S/N). Nyquist: C = 2 * B * log_2(L).

formulas.title: DBMS: Normalization
formulas.content: 1NF (Atomic), 2NF (No partial dependency), 3NF (No transitive dependency), BCNF (Determinant must be Key).

formulas.title: OS: Effective Access Time
formulas.content: EAT = (1-p) * m + p * f. Where p is page fault rate, m is memory access time, f is fault overhead.

formulas.title: Algo: Complexity Classes
formulas.content: P ⊆ NP ⊆ NP-Complete ⊆ NP-Hard. SAT is the first NP-Complete problem.

formulas.title: DS: Heap Build
formulas.content: Building a heap from scratch takes O(n) time, not n log n.

formulas.title: Networks: TCP Header
formulas.content: Minimum header size is 20 bytes. Maximum with options is 60 bytes.

formulas.title: OS: Banker's Safety
formulas.content: Need[i][j] = Max[i][j] - Allocation[i][j]. Check if Need <= Available.
